reviewerNotes: Approved as an official NIOSH/CDC Stacks FACE record. Evidence identifies a warehouse worker fatality after falling 12 feet from a pallet raised on forklift forks.
approvedSourceTitle: NIOSH FACE: Warehouse Worker Dies After Falling 12 Feet From A Pallet Raised On A Forklift Truck
approvedControls: Provide fall protection for elevated work

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official NIOSH/CDC Stacks FACE record. Evidence identifies a construction worker killed when a trench collapsed.
approvedSourceTitle: NIOSH FACE: Construction Worker Killed When Trench Collapsed
approvedControls: Keep workers out of unprotected trenches

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official NIOSH/CDC Stacks FACE record. Evidence identifies a forklift driver pinned under an overturned forklift.
approvedSourceTitle: NIOSH FACE: Forklift Driver Pinned Under an Overturned Forklift Dies
approvedControls: Train forklift operators on overturn hazards

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official CSB investigation page. Evidence identifies a July 31, 2024 ammonia release at Cuisine Solutions in Sterling, Virginia, with multiple hospital evaluations.
approvedSourceTitle: CSB Investigation: Cuisine Solutions Ammonia Release
approvedControls: Strengthen emergency response and ventilation controls

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official CSB investigation page. Evidence identifies combustible dust explosions and fire at Didion Milling that killed five workers and injured fourteen.
approvedSourceTitle: CSB Investigation: Didion Milling Company Explosion and Fire
approvedControls: Maintain explosion prevention and housekeeping programs

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official CSB investigation page. Evidence identifies a pressure vessel explosion at Loy-Lange Box Company that killed a worker and members of the public.
approvedSourceTitle: CSB Investigation: Loy-Lange Box Company Pressure Vessel Explosion
approvedControls: Verify mechanical integrity of pressure systems

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official CSB investigation page. Evidence identifies a massive explosion and fire at AB Specialty Silicones that killed four workers.
approvedSourceTitle: CSB Investigation: AB Specialty Silicones, LLC
approvedControls: Strengthen hazard analysis and emergency planning

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official NIOSH/CDC Stacks FACE record. Evidence identifies a confined space incident killing a company employee and a rescuing firefighter.
approvedSourceTitle: NIOSH FACE: Confined Space Incident Kills Two Workers – Company Employee and Rescuing Fireman
approvedControls: Prevent unplanned rescue entry without proper equipment

reviewerNotes: Approved as an official NIOSH/CDC Stacks FACE record. Evidence identifies a machine operator who died after his head was crushed in a machine.
approvedSourceTitle: NIOSH FACE: Machine Operator Dies After Head Crushed in Machine
approvedControls: Use lockout before clearing or servicing equipment
Train operators on machine hazard zones
